ui.label: Feature-Specific Covariate Path
ui.placeholder: Path to tab-delimited file or leave blank…
ui.helpText: Optional. A table with features as columns and samples as rows. Must match the row/column names of input_data.

ui.label: Feature-Specific Covariate Name
ui.helpText: Name used for the covariate when fitting per-feature models.

ui.label: Keep covariate in outputs (p-values, results, plots)
Normalization
ui.helpText: Applied to features before transformation. Default: TSS (total-sum scaling).

ui.label: Transformation
ui.helpText: Applied after normalization. LOG (base 2) is recommended. PLOG uses a pseudo-count of half the minimum non-zero abundance.

ui.label: Multiple Testing Correction
ui.helpText: FDR correction method applied to raw p-values. Default: BH (Benjamini–Hochberg).

ui.label: Evaluate Only
ui.helpText: Fit only the abundance models, only the prevalence models, or both (default).

ui.label: Minimum Abundance
ui.helpText: Features must exceed this abundance in at least min_prevalence of samples. Default: 0.

ui.label: Minimum Prevalence
ui.helpText: Minimum fraction of samples that must exceed min_abundance. Default: 0.

ui.label: Maximum Prevalence
ui.helpText: Features present in fewer samples than this fraction are excluded. Default: 1.01.

ui.label: Maximum Significance (q-value threshold)
ui.helpText: FDR-corrected q-value threshold for significance. Default: 0.1.

ui.label: Zero Threshold
ui.helpText: Abundances ≤ this value are treated as zeros. Use to suppress low-abundance false positives. Default: 0.

ui.label: Minimum Variance
ui.helpText: Features with variance ≤ this value are dropped (e.g. entirely-zero features). Default: 0.

ui.label: Standardize continuous metadata (z-scores)
ui.helpText: Recommended — enables coefficient comparison across metadata variables.

ui.label: Warn when prevalence associations may be induced by abundance
Augment data to avoid linear separability
ui.helpText: Adds small synthetic observations to prevent linear separability in logistic regression.

ui.label: Replace small random effects with fixed effects automatically
ui.helpText: Handles low numbers of observations per random-effect group in the prevalence model.

ui.label: Absolute Abundance Source
ui.helpText: If you have absolute abundance data, select how it was obtained. When using spike-in or total scaling, set median_comparison_abundance to OFF.

ui.label: Unscaled Abundance File Path
ui.placeholder: Path to tab-delimited file with one column…
ui.helpText: Single-column table with sample rows. For spike-ins, column name = spike-in feature name. For total scaling, column name = "total".

ui.label: Median comparison — abundance
ui.helpText: Test abundance coefficients against the median coefficient across features (recommended for relative abundance data). Turn OFF for absolute abundance data.

ui.label: Median Comparison Abundance Threshold
ui.helpText: Coefficients within this distance of the median are set to p = 1 (likely compositionality artefacts). Default: 0.

ui.label: Median comparison — prevalence
ui.helpText: Test prevalence coefficients against the median. OFF by default; use only if compositionality-induced sparsity is expected.

ui.label: Median Comparison Prevalence Threshold
ui.helpText: Same as the abundance threshold but applied to prevalence coefficients. Default: 0.

ui.label: Subtract median from coefficients in output
Generate summary plot of significant associations
Summary Plot — Top N Features
ui.helpText: Number of top features with significant associations to include in the summary plot. Default: 25.

ui.label: Per-Association Plots — Maximum Count
ui.helpText: Top N significant associations for which individual plots are generated. Default: 30.

ui.label: Save fitted models to RData file
